title: Ranking the Patriots’ 2026 schedule from least to most exciting games
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots’ 2026 schedule includes games against eight teams that made the 2025 playoffs.
The Patriots will play internationally in Germany and face teams like the Seahawks and Chiefs in primetime.
New England has the sixth-hardest schedule in the NFL, based on opponents' 2025 winning percentages.

title: Lakeville police investigating pipe bomb found near town pond
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lakeville police are investigating a PVC pipe bomb found near Egger Bog Pond off Highland Road.
Massachusetts State Police Bomb Squad confirmed and safely disposed of the device on Saturday.
Officials request information from the public to aid in the ongoing investigation.

title: Mass. McDonald’s employee to face criminal charges after viral video shows alleged food contamination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A Southbridge McDonald's employee is charged after a video showed her putting fries from her mouth into a serving container.
Southbridge Police confirmed criminal charges are filed after the viral video, with more details to follow after arraignment.
The employee and involved individuals have left the franchise; local authorities found no public health concerns so far.

title: Boston man awaiting trial for allegedly dumping woman’s body found guilty of rape, strangulation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rinnyers Pena, 47, convicted of rape and strangulation, acquitted of kidnapping, following charges from 2021 and 2025.
Pena found not guilty on assault and kidnapping charges but guilty on six, including rape, strangulation, and drugging.
Unrelated case pending against Pena for allegedly disinterring Alenny Matos's body; plea deal decision still pending.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 2-1 win over Minnesota United
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Revolution defeated Minnesota United 2-1 with goals from Carles Gil and Luca Langoni at Gillette Stadium.
Revolution benefited from a pressing system, converting a turnover into Langoni's decisive goal early in the second half.
Goalkeeper Matt Turner redeemed himself with crucial saves, impacting New England's defensive efficiency after a recent mistake.

title: SNL recap: Best moments from Will Ferrell hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’ season finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Will Ferrell hosted the season 51 finale of 'Saturday Night Live' on May 16, 2026, with Paul McCartney as musical guest.
Ferrell performed as the ghost of Jeffrey Epstein in the cold open alongside James Austin Johnson's Donald Trump.
Paul McCartney performed three songs and appeared in multiple sketches, including one as a British mechanic.

title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 will feature tall ships from over 25 countries as part of the Sail250 tour.
Past Sail Boston events occurred in 1980, 1992, 2000, 2009, 2012, and 2017, drawing millions of spectators.
The 1992 and 2000 events faced protests; some viewed ships as reminders of slavery's historical transport routes.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel returns to Patriots rookie minicamp, bringing renewed energy to the team ahead of the 2026 season.
The Patriots are reportedly trading for wide receiver A.J. Brown to bolster their roster for the upcoming games.
Kevin Byard expresses confidence in Vrabel's coaching, noting his consistent dynamic presence from previous coaching experience.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a go-ahead 426-foot home run to lift the Red Sox past the Braves 3-2.
Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle pitched eight innings, allowing two runs, four hits, and striking out three.
Closer Aroldis Chapman secured the victory with a save in the ninth, overcoming a bases-loaded situation.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox shortstop Trevor Story placed on 10-day injured list with a sports hernia.
Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A Worcester as Story's replacement on the roster.
Trevor Story and team are evaluating the need for potential hernia surgery.
